description: A stretched cord that produces sound when plucked or bowed; the vibrating line of stringed instruments. 'Lina' names what sings when touched—tension becoming tone.
sound_symbolism: The word vibrates. 'Li' hums with vibrating line, string responding. Then 'na' resonates in sustained note, sound continuing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo lina' (the string). For instruments—guitar, violin, harp. Different from 'sima' (thread) which is for sewing.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Resonance as beauty—the string transforms touch into tone, mathematics becoming music.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Strings connected cultures—lyre, sitar, koto, each culture finding music in stretched cord.
